title: SPF (Sender Policy Framework)
description: Async check: queries TXT records at the apex for `v=spf1` and reports presence, weak mechanisms (+all), and lookup count.
evidence: Executed via dns/promises.resolveTxt.
riskImpact: Without SPF, attackers can spoof emails from your domain (phishing / BEC).
explanation: SPF is the oldest of the three email-authentication mechanisms (SPF, DKIM, DMARC).
fixSteps: Publish a TXT record at the apex: v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com -all
Stay under the 10-lookup limit (use ip4/ip6 for static ranges).
Use -all (hard fail) or ~all (soft fail).

title: DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication)
description: Async check: queries TXT records at _dmarc.<domain> for `v=DMARC1` and reports the policy (none/quarantine/reject).
evidence: Executed via dns/promises.resolveTxt on _dmarc.<domain>.
riskImpact: Without DMARC, receivers have no policy for handling SPF/DKIM failures.
explanation: DMARC ties SPF and DKIM together with a policy. p=reject is the gold standard.
fixSteps: Add a TXT record at _dmarc.<domain>: v=DMARC1; p=reject; rua=mailto:...
Start at p=none to monitor, then move to p=quarantine and finally p=reject.

title: DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail)
description: Async check: tries a list of common DKIM selectors and reports whether any DKIM TXT or CNAME was found.
evidence: Executed via dns/promises.resolveTxt / resolveCname for ~20 selectors.
riskImpact: Without DKIM, receivers cannot verify message integrity end-to-end.
explanation: DKIM adds a cryptographic signature to every outgoing message. Selectors are provider-specific.
fixSteps: Enable DKIM signing in your mail provider (Google Workspace, M365, Fastmail, etc.).
Publish the public key as selector._domainkey.<domain> TXT.

title: DNSSEC
description: Async check: queries Google and Cloudflare DNS-over-HTTPS for the AD (Authenticated Data) flag and reports whether the chain is DNSSEC-signed.
evidence: Executed via dns.google/resolve and cloudflare-dns.com/dns-query.
riskImpact: Without DNSSEC, an on-path attacker can forge DNS responses (cache poisoning).
explanation: DNSSEC adds cryptographic signatures so resolvers can detect forged answers.
fixSteps: Enable DNSSEC at your registrar (most support one-click activation).
Verify with: dig +dnssec <your domain>

title: MTA-STS (SMTP Strict Transport Security)
description: Async check: probes _mta-sts.<domain> for an `v=STSv1` TXT record and fetches the policy file at mta-sts.<domain>/.well-known/mta-sts.txt.
evidence: DNS TXT query + HTTPS GET on the policy URL.
riskImpact: Without MTA-STS, attackers can downgrade SMTP STARTTLS or intercept mail in transit.
explanation: MTA-STS tells receiving mail servers to require TLS and refuse downgraded sessions.
fixSteps: Publish _mta-sts.<domain> TXT with v=STSv1; id=<timestamp>
Serve the policy at https://mta-sts.<domain>/.well-known/mta-sts.txt
Submit to the TLSRPT reporting address.

codeExamples.label: mta-sts.txt
codeExamples.code:
version: STSv1
mode: enforce
mx: *.example.com
max_age: 86400

title: TLS-RPT (TLS Reporting)
description: Async check: probes _smtp._tls.<domain> for a `v=TLSRPTv1` TXT record.
evidence: DNS TXT query only.
riskImpact: Without TLSRPT you get no telemetry about SMTP TLS failures.
explanation: TLSRPT is a companion to MTA-STS: it tells receivers where to send aggregate reports.
fixSteps: Publish _smtp._tls.<domain> TXT with v=TLSRPTv1; rua=https://...

title: SPF Exceeds 10 DNS Lookup Limit
description: SPF records that trigger more than 10 DNS lookups fail with a permerror.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-spf-lookup-count-too-high.
riskImpact: Use ip4: / ip6: instead of include: for static ranges; flatten nested includes
explanation: SPF records that trigger more than 10 DNS lookups fail with a permerror.
fixSteps: Use ip4: / ip6: instead of include: for static ranges; flatten nested includes

title: SPF Redirect Loop
description: SPF redirect= with a chain that loops back to the original domain fails permerror.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-spf-redirect-loop.
riskImpact: Audit the redirect chain and break the loop
explanation: SPF redirect= with a chain that loops back to the original domain fails permerror.
fixSteps: Audit the redirect chain and break the loop

title: SPF ptr Mechanism Present
description: The ptr mechanism is deprecated and SHOULD NOT be used.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-spf-ptr-mechanism.
riskImpact: Remove ptr from your SPF record
explanation: The ptr mechanism is deprecated and SHOULD NOT be used.
fixSteps: Remove ptr from your SPF record

title: DMARC Aggregate Report (rua) Missing
description: Without rua= you receive no aggregate reports, leaving you blind to spoofing attempts.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-dmarc-rua-missing.
riskImpact: Add rua=mailto:dmarc-reports@yourdomain.com (or use a DMARC analyzer)
explanation: Without rua= you receive no aggregate reports, leaving you blind to spoofing attempts.
fixSteps: Add rua=mailto:dmarc-reports@yourdomain.com (or use a DMARC analyzer)

title: DMARC Forensic Report (ruf) Missing
description: ruf= sends sample failed messages. Privacy tradeoff; enable with caution.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-dmarc-ruf-missing.
riskImpact: Add ruf=mailto:dmarc-forensic@yourdomain.com (or skip for privacy)
explanation: ruf= sends sample failed messages. Privacy tradeoff; enable with caution.
fixSteps: Add ruf=mailto:dmarc-forensic@yourdomain.com (or skip for privacy)

title: DMARC pct < 100
description: DMARC pct= applies the policy to a percentage of traffic. < 100 means some spoofed mail still gets delivered.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-dmarc-pct-not-100.
riskImpact: Set pct=100 once you're confident in the policy
explanation: DMARC pct= applies the policy to a percentage of traffic. < 100 means some spoofed mail still gets delivered.
fixSteps: Set pct=100 once you're confident in the policy

title: DKIM Signature (s=) Missing
description: DKIM records without s= are not signing anything.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-dkim-sig-tag-missing.
riskImpact: Verify your mail server is signing with s= field
explanation: DKIM records without s= are not signing anything.
fixSteps: Verify your mail server is signing with s= field

title: BIMI Record Missing
description: BIMI (Brand Indicators for Message Identification) displays your brand logo in supported mail clients.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-bimi-record-missing.
riskImpact: Publish a BIMI TXT record at default._bimi.yourdomain.com
explanation: BIMI (Brand Indicators for Message Identification) displays your brand logo in supported mail clients.
fixSteps: Publish a BIMI TXT record at default._bimi.yourdomain.com

title: MTA-STS Policy File Missing
description: MTA-STS tells receivers to require TLS. Without a policy file, mail may be downgraded.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-mta-sts-policy-missing.
riskImpact: Serve https://mta-sts.yourdomain.com/.well-known/mta-sts.txt with mode: enforce
explanation: MTA-STS tells receivers to require TLS. Without a policy file, mail may be downgraded.
fixSteps: Serve https://mta-sts.yourdomain.com/.well-known/mta-sts.txt with mode: enforce

title: TLS-RPT rua= Missing
description: TLS-RPT sends aggregate reports about SMTP TLS failures to your mailbox.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-tls-rpt-rua-missing.
riskImpact: Publish _smtp._tls.yourdomain.com TXT with v=TLSRPTv1; rua=mailto:...
explanation: TLS-RPT sends aggregate reports about SMTP TLS failures to your mailbox.
fixSteps: Publish _smtp._tls.yourdomain.com TXT with v=TLSRPTv1; rua=mailto:...

title: SMTP Open Relay Detected
description: Open SMTP relays are abused by spammers and put you on every RBL.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-smtp-open-relay.
riskImpact: Require authentication for outbound relay; disable open relay in your MTA
explanation: Open SMTP relays are abused by spammers and put you on every RBL.
fixSteps: Require authentication for outbound relay; disable open relay in your MTA

title: SMTP Banner Discloses Version
description: SMTP 220 banner includes the daemon and version. Disable the disclosure.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-smtp-banner-disclosure.
riskImpact: Set smtpd_banner to a generic string in Postfix
explanation: SMTP 220 banner includes the daemon and version. Disable the disclosure.
fixSteps: Set smtpd_banner to a generic string in Postfix

title: ARC (Authenticated Received Chain) Missing
description: ARC preserves SPF/DKIM/DMARC results across intermediaries (mailing lists, forwarding). Without ARC, legitimate forwarded mail often fails DMARC.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-arc-record-missing.
riskImpact: Mail forwarded through lists breaks DMARC and may be rejected or quarantined, hurting deliverability for legitimate users.
explanation: ARC preserves SPF/DKIM/DMARC results across intermediaries (mailing lists, forwarding). Without ARC, legitimate forwarded mail often fails DMARC.
fixSteps: Enable ARC signing on your mail transfer agents (Postfix via spamassassin/arc, Microsoft 365 has it built in).
Tune ARC seals so receivers trust the chain.

title: MTA-STS Mode 'none' in Production
description: MTA-STS policies with mode: none or mode: testing do not enforce TLS; downgrade attacks remain possible.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-mta-sts-mode-none.
riskImpact: Attackers can downgrade or intercept SMTP connections, leading to credential or content disclosure.
explanation: MTA-STS policies with mode: none or mode: testing do not enforce TLS; downgrade attacks remain possible.
fixSteps: Move to mode: enforce once you have confirmed TLS is consistently working.
Monitor TLS-RPT reports before flipping to enforce.

title: MTA-STS Policy ID Not Rotated
description: The MTA-STS id= field must be rotated to invalidate cached policies and force receivers to fetch the new policy.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-mta-sts-id-not-rotated.
riskImpact: A stale id prevents you from updating the policy in a timely way during incidents.
explanation: The MTA-STS id= field must be rotated to invalidate cached policies and force receivers to fetch the new policy.
fixSteps: Update the id= to a fresh timestamp whenever the policy changes (RFC 8461 §3.2).
Use a UTC timestamp string or counter that increments on each change.

title: BIMI Without VMC Certificate
description: BIMI logos are only displayed by Gmail and a few other receivers when accompanied by a Verified Mark Certificate (VMC) issued to the trademark holder.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-bimi-without-vmc.
riskImpact: Without a VMC, BIMI is ignored by major providers; brand display uplift is not realized.
explanation: BIMI logos are only displayed by Gmail and a few other receivers when accompanied by a Verified Mark Certificate (VMC) issued to the trademark holder.
fixSteps: Obtain a VMC from DigiCert or Entrust after your trademark is registered.
Host the VMC PEM at the URL in your BIMI record.

title: BIMI evidence= Without sha256 Hash
description: BIMI evidence URLs that omit the hash selector cannot be integrity-checked by receivers, weakening trust in the displayed logo.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-bimi-evidence-without-hash.
riskImpact: An attacker who hijacks the evidence URL can swap the logo and impersonate your brand in recipient inboxes.
explanation: BIMI evidence URLs that omit the hash selector cannot be integrity-checked by receivers, weakening trust in the displayed logo.
fixSteps: Append the sha256 fragment to your evidence URL (e.g., logo.svg#sha256=...).
Compute the hash with openssl dgst -sha256 logo.svg.

title: MX Hostname Is a CNAME (RFC Violation)
description: RFC 5321 §5.1 states MX targets must have A/AAAA records, not CNAMEs. Many relays still violate this; deliverability suffers.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-mx-hostname-cname.
riskImpact: Mail servers that strictly follow RFC 5321 will reject or de-prioritize messages to CNAME MX targets.
explanation: RFC 5321 §5.1 states MX targets must have A/AAAA records, not CNAMEs. Many relays still violate this; deliverability suffers.
fixSteps: Resolve any MX CNAMEs to the underlying A/AAAA and publish the A/AAAA directly.
Update your provider's MX records to point at the IP-shaped hostname.

title: MX Hostname Lacks A/AAAA Fallback
description: An MX target that only resolves via CNAME chain or only via AAAA is fragile; if the upstream fails, mail cannot be queued.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-mx-no-aaaa-backup.
riskImpact: Single-stack (IPv4-only or IPv6-only) MX targets cause delayed or failed delivery for clients on the other protocol.
explanation: An MX target that only resolves via CNAME chain or only via AAAA is fragile; if the upstream fails, mail cannot be queued.
fixSteps: Publish both A and AAAA records for every MX hostname.
Test with dig +short MX, dig +short A, dig +short AAAA for each target.

title: SPF include: Without Provider _spf Prefix
description: Legitimate ESPs publish their SPF records under predictable _spf.* subdomains. Includes that do not match this convention are either misconfigured or impersonating.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-spf-include-no-prefix.
riskImpact: Mis-prefixed SPF includes break the lookup chain silently and can let spoofed mail pass.
explanation: Legitimate ESPs publish their SPF records under predictable _spf.* subdomains. Includes that do not match this convention are either misconfigured or impersonating.
fixSteps: Verify each include: points at a recognized provider (google.com, sendgrid.net, mailgun.org, etc.).
Replace ad-hoc includes with the provider's documented _spf subdomain.

title: PLAIN or LOGIN SMTP AUTH Advertised
description: SMTP AUTH mechanisms PLAIN and LOGIN transmit credentials base64-encoded but unencrypted. If STARTTLS isn't enforced, passwords are trivially recoverable.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-smtp-plain-login-auth.
riskImpact: On a downgrade attack or opportunistic-TLS-fail, attackers capture plaintext credentials for your outbound relay.
explanation: SMTP AUTH mechanisms PLAIN and LOGIN transmit credentials base64-encoded but unencrypted. If STARTTLS isn't enforced, passwords are trivially recoverable.
fixSteps: Disable PLAIN and LOGIN in your MTA; require only CRAM-MD5 or SCRAM-SHA-1 over an enforced TLS channel.
Pair with MTA-STS mode: enforce so downgrades are rejected.

title: SMTP STARTTLS Not Advertised
description: A CAPABILITY response that omits STARTTLS tells clients encryption is unavailable, forcing plaintext SMTP for all traffic to that MX.
evidence: Async / body / header check for email-smtp-no-starttls.
riskImpact: All inbound and outbound mail to this MX is sent unencrypted, exposing headers, content, and credentials on the wire.
explanation: A CAPABILITY response that omits STARTTLS tells clients encryption is unavailable, forcing plaintext SMTP for all traffic to that MX.
fixSteps: Enable STARTTLS in Postfix (smtpd_tls_security_level = may) or your MTA equivalent.
Pair with MTA-STS enforce and a modern TLS certificate.
